title: Script P1: Pre-Processing Samples
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4f4nt","text":"This protocol provides methods for quality control of metagenomic data. Included is adapter trimming, quality trimming, decontamination, negative control removal, and pre-processing results. Based on the methods found in the following publication:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9mag6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8gkr3","text":"Hannigan, Geoffrey D., et al. \"The Human Skin Double-Stranded DNA Virome: Topographical and Temporal Diversity, Genetic Enrichment, and Dynamic Associations with the Host Microbiome.\" mBio 6.5 (2015): e01578-15.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":184,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"c44cj","text":"Perl scripts and other supplementary information available at:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1ti9l","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8ek4q","text":"https://figshare.com/articles/The_Human_Skin_dsDNA_Virome_Topographical_and_Temporal_Diversity_Genetic_Enrichment_and_Dynamic_Associations_with_the_Host_Microbiome/1281248","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":171}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"81EB66E00E83455FB167A6EB2A64F810","url":"https://figshare.com/articles/The_Human_Skin_dsDNA_Virome_Topographical_and_Temporal_Diversity_Genetic_Enrichment_and_Dynamic_Associations_with_the_Host_Microbiome/1281248"}}}}
steps: Make directory for the output. mkdir ./fastq_trimmed_adaptersmkdir ./fastq_trimmed_adapters_STDERR mkdir ./fastq_trimmed_adapters mkdir ./fastq_trimmed_adapters_STDERR

Write function to trim adapters. cutadapt 1.4.1 Unix https://github.com/marcelm/cutadapt/tree/25fa1e828b0f737dc43b7aec9f29582fbcb0245e/cutadapt Marcel Martin runCutadaptWithMap() {echo Input fastq = ${1} #1 = full path to fastq file to be trimmedecho Mapping file = ${2} #2 = full path to mapping file mapping fileecho Output file = ${3} #3 = full path for output directoryecho STDERR file = ${4} #4 = full path to sterr result directoryexport SAMPLEID=$(echo ${1} | sed 's/^.*\///g' | sed 's/_R.*$//')export THREEPRIME=$(awk --assign sampleid=$SAMPLEID '$1 == sampleid { print $13 }' ${2})export FIVEPRIME=$(awk --assign sampleid=$SAMPLEID '$1 == sampleid { print $12 }' ${2})/data/apps/bin/cutadapt --error-rate=0.1 --overlap=10 -a $THREEPRIME -a $FIVEPRIME ${1} > ${3} 2> ${4}gzip ${3}} This function will remove adapters with an error rate of 0.1 and overlap of 10. runCutadaptWithMap() {echo Input fastq = ${1} #1 = full path to fastq file to be trimmedecho Mapping file = ${2} #2 = full path to mapping file mapping fileecho Output file = ${3} #3 = full path for output directoryecho STDERR file = ${4} #4 = full path to sterr result directoryexport SAMPLEID=$(echo ${1} | sed 's/^.*\///g' | sed 's/_R.*$//')export THREEPRIME=$(awk --assign sampleid=$SAMPLEID '$1 == sampleid { print $13 }' ${2})export FIVEPRIME=$(awk --assign sampleid=$SAMPLEID '$1 == sampleid { print $12 }' ${2})/data/apps/bin/cutadapt --error-rate=0.1 --overlap=10 -a $THREEPRIME -a $FIVEPRIME ${1} > ${3} 2> ${4}gzip ${3} } This function will remove adapters with an error rate of 0.1 and overlap of 10.
Set the function to be used in loops. export -f runCutadaptWithMap

Trim the adapters from the fastq files of interest. ls ./raw_fastq/ | xargs -I {} --max-procs=8 sh -c 'runCutadaptWithMap ./raw_fastq/{} SkinMet_Virome_metadata.tsv ./fastq_trimmed_adapters/{} ./fastq_trimmed_adapters_STDERR/{}' raw_fastq is the directory name where the fastq files are found. Rename as needed.

Remove sequences with a quality score < 33 from the adapter trimmed sequences using the fastx toolkit. FASTX Toolkit 0.0.14 Unix https://github.com/agordon/fastx_toolkit Hannon Lab mkdir ./trimmed_fastq/ls ./fastq_trimmed_adapters/* | sed -e 's/^.*\/.*\///g' | xargs -I {} --max-procs=18 fastq_quality_trimmer -t 33 -Q 33 -i ./fastq_trimmed_adapters/{} -o ./trimmed_fastq/{} mkdir ./trimmed_fastq/ ls ./fastq_trimmed_adapters/* | sed -e 's/^.*\/.*\///g' | xargs -I {} --max-procs=18 fastq_quality_trimmer -t 33 -Q 33 -i ./fastq_trimmed_adapters/{} -o ./trimmed_fastq/{}

Remove sequencing mapping to the human genome using the DeconSeq toolkit with default parameters and the human reference GRCh37. DeconSeq toolkit 0.4.3 Unix http://sourceforge.net/projects/deconseq/files/ Schmieder R and Edwards R mkdir ./deconseq_fastqls ./trimmed_fastq/* | sed -e 's/^.*\/.*\///g' | xargs -I {} --max-procs=128 perl deconseq-standalone-0.4.3/deconseq.pl -f ./trimmed_fastq/${} -dbs hsref -out_dir ./deconseq_fastq/${}wait mkdir ./deconseq_fastq ls ./trimmed_fastq/* | sed -e 's/^.*\/.*\///g' | xargs -I {} --max-procs=128 perl deconseq-standalone-0.4.3/deconseq.pl -f ./trimmed_fastq/${} -dbs hsref -out_dir ./deconseq_fastq/${} wait

Automatically rename the output files so that they are specific for each sample. mkdir ./clean_fastqmkdir ./cont_fastqls -d ./deconseq_fastq/* | sed 's/^.*\///g' | xargs -I {} --max-procs=1 sh -c 'cp ./deconseq_fastq/{}/*_clean.fq ./clean_fastq/{}'ls -d ./deconseq_fastq/* | sed 's/^.*\///g' | xargs -I {} --max-procs=1 sh -c 'cp ./deconseq_fastq/{}/*_cont.fq ./cont_fastq/{}' Here we only rename the 'clean' files because those are the ones we will continue to work with downstream. mkdir ./clean_fastq mkdir ./cont_fastq ls -d ./deconseq_fastq/* | sed 's/^.*\///g' | xargs -I {} --max-procs=1 sh -c 'cp ./deconseq_fastq/{}/*_clean.fq ./clean_fastq/{}' ls -d ./deconseq_fastq/* | sed 's/^.*\///g' | xargs -I {} --max-procs=1 sh -c 'cp ./deconseq_fastq/{}/*_cont.fq ./cont_fastq/{}' Here we only rename the 'clean' files because those are the ones we will continue to work with downstream.

Since a 1% spike-in of PhiX Control was added to the Illumina sequencing runs for quality control purposes, any sequences mapping to the PhiX174 genome (NCBI Accession: NC_001422) are also removed using DeconSeq, but only for the whole metagenome samples (virome samples were subjected to further background removal).
Build PhiX database in deconseq-standalone-0.4.3/db bwa-0.5.9/bwa64 index −p PhiX174 PhiX174.fasta

Make directory for PhiX decontaminated output. mkdir ./deconseq_phix_fastqls ./clean_fastq/* | sed -e 's/^.*\/.*\///g' | xargs -I {} --max-procs=128 perl deconseq-standalone-0.4.3/deconseq.pl -f ./clean_fastq/{} -dbs phix -out_dir ./deconseq_phix_fastq/{}wait mkdir ./deconseq_phix_fastq ls ./clean_fastq/* | sed -e 's/^.*\/.*\///g' | xargs -I {} --max-procs=128 perl deconseq-standalone-0.4.3/deconseq.pl -f ./clean_fastq/{} -dbs phix -out_dir ./deconseq_phix_fastq/{} wait

Automatically rename the output files so that they are specific for each sample. mkdir ./clean_phix_fastqmkdir ./cont_phix_fastqls -d ./deconseq_phix_fastq/* | sed 's/^.*\///g' | xargs -I {} --max-procs=1 sh -c 'cp ./deconseq_phix_fastq/{}/*_clean.fq ./clean_phix_fastq/{}'ls -d ./deconseq_phix_fastq/* | sed 's/^.*\///g' | xargs -I {} --max-procs=1 sh -c 'cp ./deconseq_phix_fastq/{}/*_cont.fq ./cont_phix_fastq/{}' Here we only rename the 'clean' files because those are the ones we will continue to work with downstream. mkdir ./clean_phix_fastq mkdir ./cont_phix_fastq ls -d ./deconseq_phix_fastq/* | sed 's/^.*\///g' | xargs -I {} --max-procs=1 sh -c 'cp ./deconseq_phix_fastq/{}/*_clean.fq ./clean_phix_fastq/{}' ls -d ./deconseq_phix_fastq/* | sed 's/^.*\///g' | xargs -I {} --max-procs=1 sh -c 'cp ./deconseq_phix_fastq/{}/*_cont.fq ./cont_phix_fastq/{}' Here we only rename the 'clean' files because those are the ones we will continue to work with downstream.
Make a new directory for fasta files to be cleaned of environmental contamination. mkdir ./clean_R1_fasta

Convert the deconseq cleaned fastq files to fasta files and deposit the sequence files into the new directory. for file in $(ls clean_phix_fastq/); do idba_ud-1.0.9/bin/fq2fa ./clean_phix_fastq/$file ./clean_R1_fasta/${file}done for file in $(ls clean_phix_fastq/); do idba_ud-1.0.9/bin/fq2fa ./clean_phix_fastq/$file ./clean_R1_fasta/${file} done

Change the file extension names. for file in $(ls clean_R1_fasta/); do mv ./clean_R1_fasta/ for file in $(ls clean_R1_fasta/); do mv ./clean_R1_fasta/"${file}" ./clean_R1_fasta/"${file/%.fastq/.fa}" done
Remove the R2 (forward) files since this script is only cleaning the R1 direction reads. rm ./clean_R1_fasta/*R2*

Format the fasta files to include the pair numbers information (1 or 2). mkdir ./clean_R1_fasta_neg_clean_formatfor file in $(ls clean_R1_fasta/); do sed s'/ /\@/g' ./clean_R1_fasta/$file > ./clean_R1_fasta_neg_clean_format/${file}done mkdir ./clean_R1_fasta_neg_clean_format for file in $(ls clean_R1_fasta/); do sed s'/ /\@/g' ./clean_R1_fasta/$file > ./clean_R1_fasta_neg_clean_format/${file} done

The formatted fasta files will be blasted against the NCBI non-redundant database with an evalue of 0.001 and only the top hit will. First create a directory for the blast results. mkdir ./blastn_nt_sequences

Blast the sequences to use downstream for negative control removal. BLAST Toolkit 2.2.0 Unix ftp://ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/blast/executables/blast+/LATEST/ NCBI run.blastn.for.neg.cleaning.against.nt () { blastn -query ./clean_R1_fasta_neg_clean_format/${1} -out ./blastn_nt_sequences/${1} -db /references/ncbi/nt -outfmt 6 -num_threads 2 -max_target_seqs 1 -evalue 1e-3}export -f run.blastn.for.neg.cleaning.against.ntls clean_R1_fasta_neg_clean_format/ | xargs -I {} --max-procs=128 sh -c 'run.blastn.for.neg.cleaning.against.nt {}'wait A simple way to parallelize this proccess on our server is to run it as a subroutine. run.blastn.for.neg.cleaning.against.nt () { blastn -query ./clean_R1_fasta_neg_clean_format/${1} -out ./blastn_nt_sequences/${1} -db /references/ncbi/nt -outfmt 6 -num_threads 2 -max_target_seqs 1 -evalue 1e-3 } export -f run.blastn.for.neg.cleaning.against.nt ls clean_R1_fasta_neg_clean_format/ | xargs -I {} --max-procs=128 sh -c 'run.blastn.for.neg.cleaning.against.nt {}' wait A simple way to parallelize this proccess on our server is to run it as a subroutine.

Rename the file extensions to be .txt for file in $(ls blastn_nt_sequences/); do mv ./blastn_nt_sequences/ for file in $(ls blastn_nt_sequences/); do mv ./blastn_nt_sequences/"${file}" ./blastn_nt_sequences/"${file/%.fa/.txt}" done

Annotate the sequences with their corresponding gi numbers. mkdir ./tmp-dir-list1mkdir ./blastn_nt_sequences_onlycp ./blastn_nt_sequences/MG*.txt ./blastn_nt_sequences_onlyfor file in $(ls ./blastn_nt_sequences_only); do sed 's/^.*gi|//' ./blastn_nt_sequences_only/$file | sed 's/|.*//'> ./tmp-dir-list1/${file}_list1done mkdir ./tmp-dir-list1 mkdir ./blastn_nt_sequences_only cp ./blastn_nt_sequences/MG*.txt ./blastn_nt_sequences_only for file in $(ls ./blastn_nt_sequences_only); do sed 's/^.*gi|//' ./blastn_nt_sequences_only/$file | sed 's/|.*//'> ./tmp-dir-list1/${file}_list1 done

Make a list of the annotated sequence numbers. mkdir ./tmp-dir-list2for file in $(ls ./blastn_nt_sequences_only); do cut -f 1 ./blastn_nt_sequences_only/$file > ./tmp-dir-list2/${file}_list2done mkdir ./tmp-dir-list2 for file in $(ls ./blastn_nt_sequences_only); do cut -f 1 ./blastn_nt_sequences_only/$file > ./tmp-dir-list2/${file}_list2 done

Merge the two lists from previous step together. mkdir tmp-dir-paired-listsfor i in $(ls ./tmp-dir-list1); dofor j in $(ls ./tmp-dir-list2); do for x in ${i::12}; do for y in ${j::12}; do if [ ./tmp-dir-paired-lists/${i}_gi_reference fi done done done done" description="" os_version="" os_name="" guid="0869DF1E9A83486C8600CB8821B80BF5" > mkdir tmp-dir-paired-lists for i in $(ls ./tmp-dir-list1); do for j in $(ls ./tmp-dir-list2); do for x in ${i::12}; do for y in ${j::12}; do if [ "$x" == "$y" ]; then paste ./tmp-dir-list2/$j ./tmp-dir-list1/$i > ./tmp-dir-paired-lists/${i}_gi_reference fi done done done done

Add > to the start of sequence names and merge gi number to the end of the sequence. mkdir ./tmp-dir-for-fafor file in $(ls ./tmp-dir-paired-lists); do sed 's/^/>/' ./tmp-dir-paired-lists/$file | sed 's/\t/\@\@/' > ./tmp-dir-for-fa/${file}_for_fadone Use double @ (@@) because the single @ was already used to designate a different separation above. mkdir ./tmp-dir-for-fa for file in $(ls ./tmp-dir-paired-lists); do sed 's/^/>/' ./tmp-dir-paired-lists/$file | sed 's/\t/\@\@/' > ./tmp-dir-for-fa/${file}_for_fa done Use double @ (@@) because the single @ was already used to designate a different separation above.

Merge the list of sequence names and gi numbers to the list of un-merged sequence names. mkdir ./tmp-dir-paired-ids-with-gifor i in $(ls ./tmp-dir-list2); dofor j in $(ls ./tmp-dir-for-fa); do for x in ${i::12}; do for y in ${j::12}; do if [ ./tmp-dir-paired-ids-with-gi/${i}_gi_reference fi done done done done" description="" os_version="" os_name="" guid="ECDABDFF4B064A978CA99E3E01B4FB15" > mkdir ./tmp-dir-paired-ids-with-gi for i in $(ls ./tmp-dir-list2); do for j in $(ls ./tmp-dir-for-fa); do for x in ${i::12}; do for y in ${j::12}; do if [ "$x" == "$y" ]; then paste ./tmp-dir-list2/$i ./tmp-dir-for-fa/$j > ./tmp-dir-paired-ids-with-gi/${i}_gi_reference fi done done done done

Add > to the start of sequence ids. mkdir ./tmp-dir-paired-ids-with-gi-for-fafor file in $(ls ./tmp-dir-paired-ids-with-gi); do sed 's/^/>/' ./tmp-dir-paired-ids-with-gi/$file > ./tmp-dir-paired-ids-with-gi-for-fa/${file}_for_fadone mkdir ./tmp-dir-paired-ids-with-gi-for-fa for file in $(ls ./tmp-dir-paired-ids-with-gi); do sed 's/^/>/' ./tmp-dir-paired-ids-with-gi/$file > ./tmp-dir-paired-ids-with-gi-for-fa/${file}_for_fa done

Convert sequence numbers to gi ID number annotations. mkdir tmp-dir-gi-contigsfor i in $(ls ./tmp-dir-paired-ids-with-gi-for-fa); dofor j in $(ls ./clean_R1_fasta_neg_clean_format); do for x in ${i::12}; do for y in ${j::12}; do if [ ./tmp-dir-gi-contigs/${i}_gi_contigs fi done done done done" description="" os_version="" os_name="" guid="C20F8675FAAA44139EC9C343FED933E4" > mkdir tmp-dir-gi-contigs for i in $(ls ./tmp-dir-paired-ids-with-gi-for-fa); do for j in $(ls ./clean_R1_fasta_neg_clean_format); do for x in ${i::12}; do for y in ${j::12}; do if [ "$x" == "$y" ]; then awk 'FNR==NR { a[$1]=$2; next } $1 in a { $1=a[$1] }1' ./tmp-dir-paired-ids-with-gi-for-fa/$i ./clean_R1_fasta_neg_clean_format/$j > ./tmp-dir-gi-contigs/${i}_gi_contigs fi done done done done

Convert fasta files to single instead of double lined (this makes for easier removal of contig sequences using grep). mkdir ./tmp-dir-for-neg-removalfor file in $(ls ./tmp-dir-gi-contigs); do paste -s -d' \n' ./tmp-dir-gi-contigs/$file > ./tmp-dir-for-neg-removal/${file}done mkdir ./tmp-dir-for-neg-removal for file in $(ls ./tmp-dir-gi-contigs); do paste -s -d' \n' ./tmp-dir-gi-contigs/$file > ./tmp-dir-for-neg-removal/${file} done

In the original study, background control samples were collected from each subject at each sampling time point. The contaminants found in the control samples at a specific time point were removed from all other samples from the same subject at that time point. The samples will need to be seperated out by patient number so that the background control sequences for each specific patient can correspond to the other samples.
Make tmp dir for cleaned fasta intermediate files. mkdir ./tmp-neg-clean

Remove singletons from the GI reference list. First generate a list of gi numbers with line counts to detect the duplicates. Do this singleton removal because sometimes reads can be incorrectly called and this can lead to a samll number of sequences from samples being binned in the negative control samples. mkdir ./tmp-dir-list1-no-singletonsfor file in $(ls ./tmp-dir-list1); do sort ./tmp-dir-list1/$file | uniq -c | sed ./tmp-dir-list1-no-singletons/${file} done wc -l ./tmp-dir-list1/* | sed "s/\.\/.*1\///" | sed "s/\.txt.*$//" | sed "s/^\s*//" | sed "s/\s/\t/" > neg_clean_with_singleton_counts.txt wc -l ./tmp-dir-list1-no-singletons/* | sed "s/\.\/.*\///" | sed "s/\.txt.*$//" | sed "s/^\s*//" | sed "s/\s/\t/" > neg_clean_without_singleton_counts.txt" description="" os_version="" os_name="" guid="8F7F0F07AB254BB1BF841AF2DA3F941E" > mkdir ./tmp-dir-list1-no-singletons for file in $(ls ./tmp-dir-list1); do sort ./tmp-dir-list1/$file | uniq -c | sed "s/^\s*//" | sed "s/\s/\t/" | grep -v -P '^1\t.*' > ./tmp-dir-list1-no-singletons/${file} done wc -l ./tmp-dir-list1/* | sed "s/\.\/.*1\///" | sed "s/\.txt.*$//" | sed "s/^\s*//" | sed "s/\s/\t/" > neg_clean_with_singleton_counts.txt wc -l ./tmp-dir-list1-no-singletons/* | sed "s/\.\/.*\///" | sed "s/\.txt.*$//" | sed "s/^\s*//" | sed "s/\s/\t/" > neg_clean_without_singleton_counts.txt

Get singleton list for reference. mkdir ./tmp-no-singleton-listfor file in $(ls ./tmp-dir-list1-no-singletons); do cut -f 2 ./tmp-dir-list1-no-singletons/$file > ./tmp-no-singleton-list/${file}done mkdir ./tmp-no-singleton-list for file in $(ls ./tmp-dir-list1-no-singletons); do cut -f 2 ./tmp-dir-list1-no-singletons/$file > ./tmp-no-singleton-list/${file} done

For the purposes of perl filtering, it will be easiest to add @@1 to the end of each sequence ID, using sed. mkdir ./tmp-dir-gi-contigs-perl-filter-formatfor file in $(ls ./tmp-dir-gi-contigs); do sed '/\@\@/!s/$/\@\@1/' ./tmp-dir-gi-contigs/$file | sed '/>/!s/@@1//' > ./tmp-dir-gi-contigs-perl-filter-format/${file}done mkdir ./tmp-dir-gi-contigs-perl-filter-format for file in $(ls ./tmp-dir-gi-contigs); do sed '/\@\@/!s/$/\@\@1/' ./tmp-dir-gi-contigs/$file | sed '/>/!s/@@1//' > ./tmp-dir-gi-contigs-perl-filter-format/${file} done

Use grep to get all of the sequence names that contain. echo Neg Cleaning - Removing negative sequences from fasta files using perl script...neg.contig.clean () { # INPUT1 = Patient number to use (ie echo Neg Cleaning - Removing negative sequences from fasta files using perl script... neg.contig.clean () { # INPUT1 = Patient number to use (ie "1") # $27 = SiteID # $30 = Time Point # $1 = Sample ID export PAT=$(awk --assign num=$1 --assign time=$2 '$27 == num && $30 == time { print $1 }' Healthy_human_virome1_mapping.tsv)

Also set which file name is the negative control sample. Note that in this case an error will return because there is no negative for patient 1 included. export NEG_PAT=$(awk --assign num=$1 --assign time=$2 '$27 == num && $29 ==
Make a directory for the specific patient info. mkdir ./patients/patient_${1}_${2}

Copy the patient files to the patient${1} directory. echo $PAT | sed 's/\ /\n/g' | xargs -I {} --max-procs=1 sh -c The sed command is needed to parse out the words found in $PAT which will be separated by spaces

Remove all of the fasta sequences that have GI numbers matching their corresponding negative control list of GI numbers. https://figshare.com/articles/The_Human_Skin_dsDNA_Virome_Topographical_and_Temporal_Diversity_Genetic_Enrichment_and_Dynamic_Associations_with_the_Host_Microbiome/1281248 The perl script filter_fasta_file.pl was used above for filtering fasta sequences based on their sequence ID (in the case above, the gi number ID) and can be found in the supplementary information. ls ./patients/patient_${1}_${2}/ | xargs -I {} --max-procs=15 sh -c ls ./patients/patient_${1}_${2}/ | xargs -I {} --max-procs=15 sh -c "perl /project/egricelab/bin/filter_fasta_file.pl -v -l ./tmp-no-singleton-list/${NEG_PAT}* -i ./patients/patient_${1}_${2}/{} -o ./tmp-neg-clean/{} -id_regex '.*@@(\d+)'" wait }
Export the above subroutine so that it can be used in xargs for faster running on our server. export -f neg.contig.clean
Make patients dif for the above function's output. mkdir ./patients

Use a loop of 20 numbers to cover all of the possible patient numbers in the function neg.contig.clean. Some of these will return ERRORS since not all 20 patients are included in the analyses, but this will push through the errors. seq 1 20 | xargs -I {} --max-procs=20 sh -c 'bsub -n 16 -K -q max_mem64 neg.contig.clean {} 1' & seq 1 20 | xargs -I {} --max-procs=20 sh -c 'bsub -n 16 -K -q max_mem64 neg.contig.clean {} 2' & seq 1 20 | xargs -I {} --max-procs=20 sh -c 'bsub -n 16 -K -q max_mem64 neg.contig.clean {} 3'wait seq 1 20 | xargs -I {} --max-procs=20 sh -c 'bsub -n 16 -K -q max_mem64 neg.contig.clean {} 1' & seq 1 20 | xargs -I {} --max-procs=20 sh -c 'bsub -n 16 -K -q max_mem64 neg.contig.clean {} 2' & seq 1 20 | xargs -I {} --max-procs=20 sh -c 'bsub -n 16 -K -q max_mem64 neg.contig.clean {} 3' wait

Return the fasta files back to their standard "no-@-symbol" format. mkdir ./negative_clean_seqsfor file in $(ls ./tmp-neg-clean); do sed 's/@@.*$//' ./tmp-neg-clean/$file | sed 's/@/ /g' > ./negative_clean_seqs/${file}done mkdir ./negative_clean_seqs for file in $(ls ./tmp-neg-clean); do sed 's/@@.*$//' ./tmp-neg-clean/$file | sed 's/@/ /g' > ./negative_clean_seqs/${file} done

Clean up the negative control cleaned file names. for file in $(ls ./negative_clean_seqs); do mv for file in $(ls ./negative_clean_seqs); do mv "./negative_clean_seqs/${file}" "./negative_clean_seqs/${file/%.txt*/.fa}" done
Merge the files for graphing in R

Use R to graph the neg clean summary info and get the summary stats. mkdir ./neg_cntrl_clean_summary_statsRscript neg_clean_count_before_after_graphing.R mkdir ./neg_cntrl_clean_summary_stats Rscript neg_clean_count_before_after_graphing.R "./sequence_counts_neg_clean_for_R.txt" "./neg_cntrl_clean_summary_stats"

We then wanted to see how many low-quality and contaminated sequences we removed from our raw sequences. In order to do so, we had to determine how many sequences were in each file. For simplicity, we are only looking at the R1 reads. mkdir ./sequence_count_stats

Count total number of raw sequences (and median sequence lengths). https://figshare.com/articles/The_Human_Skin_dsDNA_Virome_Topographical_and_Temporal_Diversity_Genetic_Enrichment_and_Dynamic_Associations_with_the_Host_Microbiome/1281248 The perl script calculate_fastq_median_length.pl is used in this step and can be obtained from the supplementary information. for file in ./raw_fastq/*_R1.fastq; do name=`echo > ./sequence_count_stats/raw_sequence_counts.tsvperl calculate_fastq_median_length.pl $file >> ./sequence_count_stats/raw_median_seq_length.tsv done" description="" os_version="" os_name="" guid="38DA550E78C342108D48AC6EB17AFB5D" > for file in ./raw_fastq/*_R1.fastq; do name=`echo "$file" | sed 's/\.\/raw_fastq\///g' | sed 's/_R1\.fastq//g'`count=`cat $file | wc -l | awk '{print $1/4}'`echo -e "$name\t$count" >> ./sequence_count_stats/raw_sequence_counts.tsvperl calculate_fastq_median_length.pl $file >> ./sequence_count_stats/raw_median_seq_length.tsv done

Count adapter trimmed (cutadapt) and quality trimmed (fastx) sequences (and median sequence lengths). for file in ./trimmed_fastq/*_R1.fastq; do name=`echo > ./sequence_count_stats/trimmed_sequence_counts.tsvperl calculate_fastq_median_length.pl $file >> ./sequence_count_stats/trimmed_median_seq_length.tsv done" description="" os_version="" os_name="" guid="B4DBD37B9D0549BEA62F642E8735B43F" > for file in ./trimmed_fastq/*_R1.fastq; do name=`echo "$file" | sed 's/\.\/trimmed_fastq\///g' | sed 's/_R1\.fastq//g'`count=`cat $file | wc -l | awk '{print $1/4}'`echo -e "$name\t$count" >> ./sequence_count_stats/trimmed_sequence_counts.tsvperl calculate_fastq_median_length.pl $file >> ./sequence_count_stats/trimmed_median_seq_length.tsv done

Count human decontaminated sequences (and median sequences lengths). for file in ./clean_fastq/*_R1.fastq; do name=`echo > ./sequence_count_stats/human_deconseq_sequence_counts.tsvperl calculate_fastq_median_length.pl $file >> ./sequence_count_stats/human_cleaned_median_seq_length.tsv done" description="" os_version="" os_name="" guid="F975CE2AFDA1473C992359F2CEC358F9" > for file in ./clean_fastq/*_R1.fastq; do name=`echo "$file" | sed 's/\.\/clean_fastq\///g' | sed 's/_R1\.fastq//g'`count=`cat $file | wc -l | awk '{print $1/4}'`echo -e "$name\t$count" >> ./sequence_count_stats/human_deconseq_sequence_counts.tsvperl calculate_fastq_median_length.pl $file >> ./sequence_count_stats/human_cleaned_median_seq_length.tsv done

Count PhiX decontaminated sequences (and median sequence lengths) (whole metagenome only). for file in ./clean_phix_fastq/*_R1.fastq; do name=`echo > ./sequence_count_stats/phix_clean_sequence_counts.tsvperl calculate_fastq_median_length.pl $file >> ./sequence_count_stats/clean_phix_median_seq_length.tsv done" description="" os_version="" os_name="" guid="BD0138E85EF5424C8DF0DB670FA09CA0" > for file in ./clean_phix_fastq/*_R1.fastq; do name=`echo "$file" | sed 's/\.\/clean_phix_fastq\///g' | sed 's/_R1\.fastq//g'`count=`cat $file | wc -l | awk '{print $1/4}'`echo -e "$name\t$count" >> ./sequence_count_stats/phix_clean_sequence_counts.tsvperl calculate_fastq_median_length.pl $file >> ./sequence_count_stats/clean_phix_median_seq_length.tsv done

Count Negative Control decontaminated sequences (and median sequence lengths) (virome only). https://figshare.com/articles/The_Human_Skin_dsDNA_Virome_Topographical_and_Temporal_Diversity_Genetic_Enrichment_and_Dynamic_Associations_with_the_Host_Microbiome/1281248 The perl script calculate_fasta_median_length.pl is used in this step and is obtainable from the supplementary information. for file in ./negative_clean_seqs/*_R1.fa; do name=`echo > ./sequence_count_stats/negative_clean_sequence_counts.tsvperl calculate_fasta_median_length.pl $file >> ./sequence_count_stats/negative_clean_sequence_length_medians.tsv done" description="" os_version="" os_name="" guid="17FA0CC17D5C4B41982F8EF274914783" > for file in ./negative_clean_seqs/*_R1.fa; do name=`echo "$file" | sed 's/\.\/negative_clean_seqs\///g' | sed 's/_R1\.fa//g'`count=`cat $file | wc -l | awk '{print $1/2}'`echo -e "$name\t$count" >> ./sequence_count_stats/negative_clean_sequence_counts.tsvperl calculate_fasta_median_length.pl $file >> ./sequence_count_stats/negative_clean_sequence_length_medians.tsv done

We further validated our database quality by comparing the levels of 16S rRNA sequences between the virome and whole metagenome, as well as comparing the number of reads matching from the virome to the whole metagenome, and vice versa. First we quantified the levels of 16S rRNA matching sequences. mkdir ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hitsmkdir ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/whole_metagenome_subsampled_10kmkdir ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/whole_metagenome_subsampled_10k_fastamkdir ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/virome_subsampled_10k Make dir for output mkdir ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits mkdir ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/whole_metagenome_subsampled_10k mkdir ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/whole_metagenome_subsampled_10k_fasta mkdir ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/virome_subsampled_10k Make dir for output
Run blastn to attempt to quantify level of contamination using gg16SrRNA database.

Subsample files. for file in $(ls /project/egricelab/SkinMetVirome_tmp_files/clean_phix_fastq/*_R1* | sed 's/.*\///g'); do NAME=$(echo ${file} | sed 's/\.fastq//') echo Subsampling ${NAME}... /project/egricelab/meiselj_software/idba_ud-1.0.9/bin/fq2fa /project/egricelab/SkinMetVirome_tmp_files/clean_phix_fastq/${NAME}.fastq ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/whole_metagenome_subsampled_10k_fasta/${NAME}.fasta /project/egricelab/meiselj_software/seqtk-master/seqtk sample ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/whole_metagenome_subsampled_10k_fasta/${NAME}.fasta 10000 > ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/whole_metagenome_subsampled_10k/${NAME}.fastadonefor file in $(ls ./negative_clean_seqs); doecho Subsampling ${file}... /project/egricelab/meiselj_software/seqtk-master/seqtk sample ./negative_clean_seqs/$file 10000 > ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/virome_subsampled_10k/${file}done for file in $(ls /project/egricelab/SkinMetVirome_tmp_files/clean_phix_fastq/*_R1* | sed 's/.*\///g'); do NAME=$(echo ${file} | sed 's/\.fastq//') echo Subsampling ${NAME}... /project/egricelab/meiselj_software/idba_ud-1.0.9/bin/fq2fa /project/egricelab/SkinMetVirome_tmp_files/clean_phix_fastq/${NAME}.fastq ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/whole_metagenome_subsampled_10k_fasta/${NAME}.fasta /project/egricelab/meiselj_software/seqtk-master/seqtk sample ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/whole_metagenome_subsampled_10k_fasta/${NAME}.fasta 10000 > ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/whole_metagenome_subsampled_10k/${NAME}.fasta done for file in $(ls ./negative_clean_seqs); doecho Subsampling ${file}... /project/egricelab/meiselj_software/seqtk-master/seqtk sample ./negative_clean_seqs/$file 10000 > ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/virome_subsampled_10k/${file} done

Move the blastx output to its own directory. echo Performing blastx for virome...mkdir ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/green_genes_blastx_hitsfor file in $(ls ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/virome_subsampled_10k); doblastn -query ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/virome_subsampled_10k/${file} -out ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/green_genes_blastx_hits/${file} -db /project/egricelab/ghanni_software/ribosome_ref_test/green_genes_for_virus_db -outfmt 6 -num_threads 2 -max_target_seqs 1 -evalue 1e-5done echo Performing blastx for virome... mkdir ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/green_genes_blastx_hits for file in $(ls ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/virome_subsampled_10k); doblastn -query ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/virome_subsampled_10k/${file} -out ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/green_genes_blastx_hits/${file} -db /project/egricelab/ghanni_software/ribosome_ref_test/green_genes_for_virus_db -outfmt 6 -num_threads 2 -max_target_seqs 1 -evalue 1e-5 done
Get the numbers of hits for the virome samples. First get the blastx hits for the virome against the greengenes database.

Get the number of sequences in each file as well. wc -l ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/virome_subsampled_10k/* | sed 's/\.\/HUMAnN_output\/neg_clean_fasta_subsampled_10k\///' | sed 's/_R1\.fa//' | grep -v total | sed 's/^\s*//' | sed 's/\s/\t/g' | awk '{ print $1/2
Paste together the files.

Run the same blast on the whole metagenome samples for a comparison. echo Estimating bacterial levels in whole metagenome using blastx against the gg16SrRNA database...mkdir ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/green_genes_blastx_hits_whole_metagenomefor file in $(ls ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/whole_metagenome_subsampled_10k); doblastn -query ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/whole_metagenome_subsampled_10k/${file} -out ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/green_genes_blastx_hits_whole_metagenome/${file} -db /project/egricelab/ghanni_software/ribosome_ref_test/green_genes_for_virus_db -outfmt 6 -num_threads 2 -max_target_seqs 1 -evalue 1e-5done echo Estimating bacterial levels in whole metagenome using blastx against the gg16SrRNA database... mkdir ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/green_genes_blastx_hits_whole_metagenome for file in $(ls ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/whole_metagenome_subsampled_10k); doblastn -query ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/whole_metagenome_subsampled_10k/${file} -out ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/green_genes_blastx_hits_whole_metagenome/${file} -db /project/egricelab/ghanni_software/ribosome_ref_test/green_genes_for_virus_db -outfmt 6 -num_threads 2 -max_target_seqs 1 -evalue 1e-5 done
Format these numbers into a table for R. First get together a list of the blastx hits against green genes.

Make list of the total number of sequences. wc -l ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/whole_metagenome_subsampled_10k/* | sed 's/\/project\/egricelab\/metagenome_subsampled_5000\///' | sed 's/_R1_subsampled\.fa//' | grep -v total | sed 's/^\s*//' | sed 's/\s/\t/g' | awk '{ print $1/2
Paste together these files.
Cat together the files for input into R.

We also quantified the number of reads mapping from one overall dataset to the other corresponding dataset (virome to whole metagenome and vice versa). mkdir ./virome_whole_metagenome_blastmkdir ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/blastdb_whole_metagenomemkdir ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/blastn_virome_resultsmkdir ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/blastdb_viromemkdir ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/blastn_whole_metagenome_resultsmkdir ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/whole_metagenome_subsampled_50kmkdir ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/virome_subsampled_50k Make directories for output. mkdir ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast mkdir ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/blastdb_whole_metagenome mkdir ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/blastn_virome_results mkdir ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/blastdb_virome mkdir ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/blastn_whole_metagenome_results mkdir ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/whole_metagenome_subsampled_50k mkdir ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/virome_subsampled_50k Make directories for output.

Subsample files. for file in $(ls ./clean_phix_fastq/*_R1* | sed 's/.*\///g'); do NAME=$(echo ${file} | sed 's/\.fastq//') echo Subsampling ${NAME}... idba_ud-1.0.9/bin/fq2fa ./clean_phix_fastq/${NAME}.fastq ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/whole_metagenome_subsampled_10k_fasta/${NAME}.fasta seqtk sample ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/whole_metagenome_subsampled_10k_fasta/${NAME}.fasta 50000 > ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/whole_metagenome_subsampled_50k/${NAME}.fastadonefor file in $(ls ./negative_clean_seqs); do echo Subsampling ${file}... seqtk sample ./negative_clean_seqs/${file} 50000 > ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/virome_subsampled_50k/${file}done for file in $(ls ./clean_phix_fastq/*_R1* | sed 's/.*\///g'); do NAME=$(echo ${file} | sed 's/\.fastq//') echo Subsampling ${NAME}... idba_ud-1.0.9/bin/fq2fa ./clean_phix_fastq/${NAME}.fastq ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/whole_metagenome_subsampled_10k_fasta/${NAME}.fasta seqtk sample ./estimated_contamination_ribosome_hits/whole_metagenome_subsampled_10k_fasta/${NAME}.fasta 50000 > ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/whole_metagenome_subsampled_50k/${NAME}.fasta done for file in $(ls ./negative_clean_seqs); do echo Subsampling ${file}... seqtk sample ./negative_clean_seqs/${file} 50000 > ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/virome_subsampled_50k/${file} done

Generate blastn reference from whole metagenome samples and perform blastn of virome reads against database. run_blastn_virome_to_wm_and_back () { if [ -f /etc/profile.d/modules.sh ]; then source /etc/profile.d/modules.sh fi module load ncbi-blast-2.2.0export NAME=$(echo $1 | sed 's/_R1\.fasta//g')echo Sample ID is ${NAME}makeblastdb -dbtype nucl -in ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/whole_metagenome_subsampled_50k/$1 -out ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/blastdb_whole_metagenome/${NAME}_db

Set the corresponding virus sample name to a new variable. export VIRUSID=$(awk --assign name=${NAME} '$5 == name { print $7 }' ./SkinMet_and_Virome_001_metadata.tsv) echo Virus Sample ID for ${NAME} is ${VIRUSID} blastn -query ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/virome_subsampled_50k/${VIRUSID}_R1.fa -out ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/blastn_virome_results/${VIRUSID}.txt -db ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/blastdb_whole_metagenome/${NAME}_db -outfmt 6 -max_target_seqs 1 -evalue 1e-5

Perform the opposite blastn as well, first making a new database. makeblastdb -dbtype nucl -in ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/virome_subsampled_50k/${VIRUSID}_R1.fa -out ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/blastdb_virome/${VIRUSID}_db blastn -query ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/whole_metagenome_subsampled_50k/$1 -out ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/blastn_whole_metagenome_results/${NAME}.txt -db ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/blastdb_virome/${VIRUSID}_db -outfmt 6 -max_target_seqs 1 -evalue 1e-5}export -f run_blastn_virome_to_wm_and_backmkdir ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/error-outls ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/whole_metagenome_subsampled_50k | xargs -I {} --max-procs=328 sh -c makeblastdb -dbtype nucl -in ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/virome_subsampled_50k/${VIRUSID}_R1.fa -out ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/blastdb_virome/${VIRUSID}_db blastn -query ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/whole_metagenome_subsampled_50k/$1 -out ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/blastn_whole_metagenome_results/${NAME}.txt -db ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/blastdb_virome/${VIRUSID}_db -outfmt 6 -max_target_seqs 1 -evalue 1e-5 } export -f run_blastn_virome_to_wm_and_back mkdir ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/error-out ls ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/whole_metagenome_subsampled_50k | xargs -I {} --max-procs=328 sh -c "run_blastn_virome_to_wm_and_back {}"

Generate a table of the blastn hit abundances for each sample, split by whether it is virome or whole metagenome. wc -l ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/blastn_virome_results/* | sed 's/^ *//' | sed 's/ /\t/' | sed 's/\.\/.*\(MG\)/\1/' | sed 's/\.txt//' | grep -v total > ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/virome_to_metagenome_blastn_results.tsvwc -l ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/blastn_whole_metagenome_results/* | sed 's/^ *//' | sed 's/ /\t/' | sed 's/\.\/.*\(MG\)/\1/' | sed 's/\.txt//' | grep -v total > ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/metagenome_to_virome_blastn_results.tsvwc -l ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/virome_subsampled_50k/* | sed 's/^ *//' | sed 's/ /\t/' | sed 's/\.\/.*\(MG\)/\1/' | sed 's/_R1\.fa//' | grep -v total > ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/virome_to_metagenome_sequence_counts.tsvwc -l ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/whole_metagenome_subsampled_50k/* | sed 's/^ *//' | sed 's/ /\t/' | sed 's/\.\/.*\(MG\)/\1/' | sed 's/_R1\.fasta//' | grep -v total > ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/metagenome_to_virome_sequence_counts.tsv wc -l ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/blastn_virome_results/* | sed 's/^ *//' | sed 's/ /\t/' | sed 's/\.\/.*\(MG\)/\1/' | sed 's/\.txt//' | grep -v total > ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/virome_to_metagenome_blastn_results.tsv wc -l ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/blastn_whole_metagenome_results/* | sed 's/^ *//' | sed 's/ /\t/' | sed 's/\.\/.*\(MG\)/\1/' | sed 's/\.txt//' | grep -v total > ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/metagenome_to_virome_blastn_results.tsv wc -l ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/virome_subsampled_50k/* | sed 's/^ *//' | sed 's/ /\t/' | sed 's/\.\/.*\(MG\)/\1/' | sed 's/_R1\.fa//' | grep -v total > ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/virome_to_metagenome_sequence_counts.tsv wc -l ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/whole_metagenome_subsampled_50k/* | sed 's/^ *//' | sed 's/ /\t/' | sed 's/\.\/.*\(MG\)/\1/' | sed 's/_R1\.fasta//' | grep -v total > ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/metagenome_to_virome_sequence_counts.tsv

Combine the sequence count and hit tables. Some of the whole metagenome samples did not have corresponding virome samples and so just had zero hits recorded. This will be cleaned up downstream in R. awk 'FNR==NR { a[$2]=$1; next } $2 in a { print $2 ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/virome_to_metagenome_shared_results.tsv awk 'FNR==NR { a[$2]=$1; next } $2 in a { print $2"\t"$1"\t"a[$2] }' ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/metagenome_to_virome_blastn_results.tsv ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/metagenome_to_virome_sequence_counts.tsv > ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/metagenome_to_virome_shared_results.tsv" description="" os_version="" os_name="" guid="5563759C3CD742A5B00DE6909F6E1410" > awk 'FNR==NR { a[$2]=$1; next } $2 in a { print $2"\t"$1"\t"a[$2] }' ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/virome_to_metagenome_blastn_results.tsv ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/virome_to_metagenome_sequence_counts.tsv > ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/virome_to_metagenome_shared_results.tsv awk 'FNR==NR { a[$2]=$1; next } $2 in a { print $2"\t"$1"\t"a[$2] }' ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/metagenome_to_virome_blastn_results.tsv ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/metagenome_to_virome_sequence_counts.tsv > ./virome_whole_metagenome_blast/metagenome_to_virome_shared_results.tsv
